Groww is one of the most beginner-friendly Android investment apps I’ve used. The interface is clean, SIP setup for mutual funds is simple, and buying stocks/ETFs is straightforward. The trade-off is that research tools and advanced trading features are not as deep as specialized trading platforms. For long-term investors and first-time users, it’s an excellent choice.

replyContent: Hey Deepak, sorry for the inconvenience, Please follow below steps to resolved this issue. On your android phone or tablet, open the setting app Tap App & notification > see all apps Scroll down and tap Google play store Tap storage > Clear cache Tap clear all data Re- open the playstore and try to download again.

replyContent: Hi Ashutosh,as per the new circular from Exchange ,if you sell stocks from your Demat or T1(BTST), going forward, only 80% credit against the sale value will be available for subsequent trades on the selling day.Earlier you were getting 100% credit on trade day, but going forward you will get 80% to T-day and rest 20% on T+1 day (next trading day)

So, it gets quite annoying if you can't remove them from there. 2. There is no option to shortlist your favourite funds. 3. Returns of funds which has been there since quite long, may be seven yrs or 10 yrs is not provided. Edit: I have a query. For starting SIP from any platform, you need to add a biller to your bank account. But Groww didn't ask me to go through that procedure. How would I be able to start a SIP without adding the biller?
replyContent:
Ben.
1.You can easily remove any fund from the watchlist, you just need swipe the fund to the left and click on the delete icon.
2.Watchlist should serve the purpose for shorlisting
3.For SIP you are given an option to either verify your mandate or add the biller. This will help you to automate your SIPs.
